title: get_blocking scoped-thread pattern avoids nested runtime panic
description: get_blocking uses std::thread::scope to spawn a scoped thread with its own runtime when called inside an existing tokio runtime. The naive alternative (tokio::runtime::Handle::block_on) panics on multi-thread runtimes and deadlocks on current-thread runtimes. The scoped-thread approach is the correct solution and avoids both failure modes. It borrows the store and name safely without lifetime transmutation because the scope guarantees joining before return. Do not replace with block_on.
